mechanism: GA 20-oxidase catalyses GA12→GA9 and GA53→GA20; rate-limiting late GA biosynthesis.
GA 3-oxidase catalyses 3β-hydroxylation GA20→GA1 producing bioactive GA.
GA 2-oxidase catabolises bioactive GA1/GA4 to inactive GA8/GA34.
TaGA1ox1 1β-hydroxylates GA9/GA20 to inactive GA61/GA60, diverting from bioactive pool.
Rht8 (TaRNHL-D1) promotes bioactive GA biosynthesis in stem.
Nitrate signalling activates GA metabolism gene expression, raising bioactive GA.
GA induces its own catabolism — classic GA homeostasis (GA→TaGA2ox↑→GA↓).
VRN1 binds and activates the TaGA20ox2 promoter, coupling vernalization to GA biosynthesis.
DELLA promotes TaGA20ox transcription (negative-feedback GA homeostasis: DELLA↑→GA↑→DELLA↓).
Rht18 allele drives ~4-fold upregulation of TaGA2ox-A9, increasing GA catabolism.
Rht24b cis-regulatory variant elevates TaGA2ox-A9 expression in stems.
Rht12b SNP-390(C/A) elevates TaGA2oxA13 expression; causal variant of the Rht12 locus.
GA binding in the TaGID1 pocket induces conformational change enabling DELLA interaction.
GA-bound TaGID1 binds Rht-B1 DELLA N-terminal motif, recruiting SCF-SLY1 for ubiquitination.
GA-bound TaGID1 binds Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog for SCF-SLY1-mediated degradation.
SCF-TaSLY1 F-box ubiquitinates Rht-B1 DELLA for 26S proteasomal degradation.
SCF-TaSLY1 ubiquitinates Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog (parallel to Rht-B1).
Nitrate promotes GA-mediated destabilisation of DELLA (partial rescue by global-della mutant).
Rht-B1 DELLA represses GA-responsive growth; Rht-B1b reduces height ~20%, Rht-B1c ~50%.
Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog represses growth; Rht-D1b Green-Revolution dwarfing allele ~20%.
DELLA GRAS PFYRE subdomain physically binds and sequesters PLATZ-A1 (Y2H + genetics).
PLATZ-A1 (Rht25) LOF reduces height ~12.7%; OE increases height (causal Rht25 gene).
DELLA sequesters PIF TFs; accumulated Rht-B1c constitutively blocks PIF activity.
PIFs activate cell-elongation genes; DELLA-sequestration decreases stem elongation.
Red/far-red light via phytochromes targets PIFs for degradation.
TaERF-A1 directly binds the TaPIF4 promoter and activates its transcription (EMSA/ChIP).
TaD11 (C6-oxidase) catalyses late BR biosynthesis step; tad11-2a reduces endogenous BR.
TaDWF4 (CYP90B) 22α-hydroxylation of campesterol — rate-limiting BR biosynthesis branch-point.
BL ligand binds TaBRI1 extracellular domain, activating receptor kinase and downstream cascade.
TaSERK (BAK1 co-receptor) heterodimerises with TaBRI1, required for BR perception.
TaBKI1 represses TaBRI1 kinase activity in the BR-off state.
Activated TaBRI1 facilitates TaZnF-mediated ubiquitination of TaBKI1 (mutual release loop).
TaZnF RING E3 ligase ubiquitinates TaBKI1 for proteasomal destruction.
BRI1-BSK-CDG-BSU1 cascade dephosphorylates and inactivates TaBIN2 (GSK3-like kinase).
TaBIN2 phosphorylates TaBZR1/TaBES1, blocking nuclear localisation and DNA binding.
Dephosphorylated TaBZR1/TaBES1 activates BR-response growth and cell-elongation genes.
TaBZR1 physically binds DELLA proteins; reciprocal sequestration (BR-GA antagonism).
TaBIN2 phosphorylates and stabilises DELLA (when BR low); BR-GA crosstalk.
Auxin binds TaTIR1/AFB F-box forming co-receptor with Aux/IAA substrate.
Auxin-loaded TaTIR1 targets TaAux/IAA proteins for proteasomal degradation.
TaAux/IAA binds and represses TaARF TFs; removal de-represses ARF activity.
TaARF4-B haplotypes associate with plant height QTL; ARFs activate growth/elongation genes.
TaARF4-A regulates TaHB33 expression (auxin-response pathway).
TaHB33 represses TaGH3 expression, indirectly raising active auxin.
TaGH3 enzymes conjugate IAA with amino acids, reducing the active auxin pool.
Cold epigenetically induces VRN1 in shoot apex.
Ppd-1 is a pseudo-response regulator entrained by day length; long-day activates Ppd-1.
Dominant Ppd-D1a shortens each internode by ~10%, reducing overall plant height.
Autoactive Rht-B13b (S240F) NB-LRR upregulates PR genes including class-III peroxidases.
Class-III peroxidases cross-link cell-wall compounds, limiting elongation and reducing height.
TaOSCA1.4 hyperosmolality-gated Ca2+ channel; RNAi reduces height 11-16% via GA-independent route.
